description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
Two small, independent fixes bundled because their touch surfaces don't overlap with each other or with S1/S2.

D5 fix (path safety still writes):
In Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.phase_json_path, on the error branch (when JsonPath.canonicalize returns the :unsafe_json_path failure), return {:abort, report, %Config{config | json_path: nil}, reason, details}. The downstream emit_abort -> DefaultIo.emit then routes the error JSON to stdout instead of the rejected path. One-line change.

D3 fix (debug log leak):
Drop the Logger.debug/1 call in MutagenEx.AstCache.log_categories/2 entirely. Coordinate: also drop the 'require Logger' directive in the same module if no other call uses it (grep confirms it's the only one).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mutagen.cli.v10 (mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only unsafe_json_path)
  - new ast_cache_test.exs test asserting capture_log around AstCache.load/2 is empty
  - existing mix test passes with no regression
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - mutagen.cli.s15 — --json /tmp/foo.json rejected; no file created at rejected path; error JSON on stdout
  - mix mutagen invocation produces no [debug] AstCache.load/2: ... line on stderr

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex run/1 body (owned by S1)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex emit_abort/5 (owned by S2)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (owned by S2)
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/** (owned by S2)
- test/integration/** (owned by S4)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex — the canonicalize logic itself is correct, only IO routing is wrong

Allowed touches:
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_json_path error branch ONLY — one-line nil-out of json_path)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (drop Logger.debug call + require Logger directive)
- test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs (additive — unsafe-path test asserts no file at rejected path)
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs (additive — no-debug-log test)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No change to JsonPath.canonicalize/2 behavior — the safety check is correct
- No change to DefaultIo.emit/3 — IO sink is correct; only the abort glue is wrong
- No new logging system or debug flag (we're deleting a stray debug log, not introducing config surface)
- No change to --unsafe-json-outside-project — the documented escape hatch stays
- No new dependencies

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Spec verification: cli.r15 stub (v10) referenced
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-path-safety-log-hygiene/.codex-runs/mutagen-wrd.39.3.log

Files touched:
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_json_path error branch: clears Config.json_path)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (drops Logger.debug + require Logger)
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md (cli.v10 stub flipped)
- test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs (additive — unsafe_json_path regression)
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs (additive — capture_log assertion)
Tests added: Mix.Tasks.MutagenTest unsafe_json_path (rejected path not written, error JSON on stdout); AstCacheTest "load/2 emits no debug log for category diagnostics"
Verification stubs flipped: cli.r15 / cli.v10 -> execute: true

Summary: D5 — phase_json_path now returns Config{json_path: nil} on :unsafe_json_path
abort so emit_abort -> DefaultIo routes the error JSON to stdout instead of the rejected
path; D3 — drops the stray Logger.debug from AstCache.log_categories/2 (and the now-unused
require Logger). mix test: 634 tests, 0 failures.

VERIFY: PASS

Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-downstream-adoption-fixes/85b2234d-9f34-4283-b82c-0e4e638329a2.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (.spec/specs/cli.spec.md — single-line execute:false→true flip on cli.v10 stub, structurally required by the ticket's own Verification line "mutagen.cli.v10 (mix test ... --only unsafe_json_path)". Stub-flip is the conventional spec touch for an implementer; advisory note.)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1, passed: 1
  - mutagen.cli.v10 (mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only unsafe_json_path): 1/1
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.cli.r15: covered by test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:5-46 ("unsafe --json path is rejected without writing to that path"). Would fail if phase_json_path returned the original config on the :unsafe_json_path abort branch — the `assert config.json_path == nil` (line 33) and `refute File.exists?(rejected_path)` (line 34) both pin the r15 contract end-to-end (config carries nil → DefaultIo.emit routes to stdout → no write to rejected path).
  - mutagen.cli.r10 (covered transitively by s15): pre-existing canonicalize logic untouched; this ticket's test exercises the abort routing path that depends on r10's rejection.
  - mutagen.coverage.r9 (only diagnostic-log surface, not a must consumer contract): D3 hygiene fix asserted by test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs:239-252 ("load/2 emits no debug log for category diagnostics"). Would fail if Logger.debug were reintroduced in AstCache.log_categories/2 — the `assert log == ""` after capture_log at debug level catches any log emission on the load path.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. JsonPath.canonicalize/2 untouched; DefaultIo.emit/3 untouched (verified — no diff on lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex or lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/default_io.ex). No new logging system, no new deps (mix.exs / mix.lock unchanged). --unsafe-json-outside-project escape hatch preserved.
- Project verification: PASS
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean (exit 0)
  - mix test: 634 tests, 0 failures, 17 excluded
  - mix test --include integration: 651 tests, 0 failures
- Audit-expanded scope: none (no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The phase_json_path edit is exactly one line in the {:error, ...} branch (519:518) — matches "phase_json_path error branch ONLY — one-line nil-out of json_path" literally.
- ast_cache.ex: `require Logger` directive AND the Logger.debug call both removed; `grep -n "Logger" lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` returns empty — coordination with the ticket's "if no other call uses it" condition is satisfied.
- emit_abort/5 (line 1223) was not modified; the fix is upstream of emit_abort (in phase_json_path) so emit_abort just sees a Config with json_path: nil and DefaultIo's existing `%Config{json_path: nil} -> IO.write(iodata)` branch (default_io.ex:37-38) handles routing. Honors "Out of Scope (files): lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex emit_abort/5 (owned by S2)".
- Test dispatch is minimal (only :scope + :io); CLI parsing and phase_json_path use defaults, so the test exercises real production code in those paths.
- Spec-file touch (.spec/specs/cli.spec.md) is a one-line stub flip on cli.v10 (execute:false → execute:true) the ticket explicitly anticipates ("Tests to pass: mutagen.cli.v10"). Reading the Allowed touches strictly, this is not enumerated; reading the ticket holistically (Verification block references the stub by id) it is implicitly required. Advisory; verdict stays PASS.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 85b2234d-9f34-4283-b82c-0e4e638329a2
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-downstream-adoption-fixes/85b2234d-9f34-4283-b82c-0e4e638329a2.jsonl

Reasoning: Re-checked the diff against the ticket independently. The phase_json_path one-line change at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:518 nils Config.json_path on the :unsafe_json_path abort branch exactly as the ticket prescribes; downstream DefaultIo.emit/3 (default_io.ex:37-38) routes %Config{json_path: nil} to IO.write/1 — that chain is preserved untouched, so the fix is load-bearing for cli.r15. The new test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:5-44 drives real production code through phase_cli + phase_json_path + emit_abort (only :scope and :io are stubbed) with a /tmp/ path that JsonPath.canonicalize rejects via inside?/2 — and the `assert config.json_path == nil` (line 34) would fail without the fix (it pins the exact one-line change). The AstCache test (ast_cache_test.exs:239-252) uses capture_log at :debug level and asserts log == "", which would fail if Logger.debug were reintroduced in log_categories/2; grep confirms zero remaining Logger references in lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex, so removing `require Logger` was correct per the coordinate clause. Out of Scope (intent) holds: lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex, lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/default_io.ex, mix.exs, mix.lock all unchanged in the diff; emit_abort/5 (line 1223) is untouched; --unsafe-json-outside-project surface is preserved. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 634/0; mix test --include integration 651/0 (the embedded "7 tests, 1 failure" lines in the integration output are downstream `mix mutagen` invocations against fixtures — expected mutation-kills, not regressions).

The .spec/specs/cli.spec.md touch is a legitimate consequential edit, not scope creep: it is a single-line `execute: false → execute: true` flip on the cli.v10 stub which the ticket's Verification block ("Tests to pass: mutagen.cli.v10") and Merge gate ("Spec verification: cli.r15 stub (v10) referenced") both name explicitly. The base commit (ce59324) introduced v10 at `execute: false` precisely because the test didn't yet exist; flipping it to true is the conventional registration of the now-wired verification surface. Allowed touches omits it literally, but the ticket holistically requires it. The verifier flagged it as advisory with sound rationale; the call stands.

title: S3: Path-safety hardening + log hygiene
